svg.text: 31-Skills: Complete Skill System — Definition, Types, Editing & Node Graphs

g.text: Skills is an encapsulation that allows a character to perform a preset behavior in response to
user input during gameplay. In classic mode, Normal Attack, Elemental Skill, and Elemental Burst

svg.text: II. Editing Skills — Entry & Skill Parameters

g.text: The Skill Editor can be accessed under the Skills tab in the Combat Preset tab.
Click [New Skill] → [Confirm Create] to add a new skill.
Configuration ID — Unique identifier, referenced in Node Graph.
Enable Fall Protection — Prevents cliff falling during displacement skills.
Allow Cast in Mid Air — Usage while jumping, falling, or gliding.
Special Status Availability — Forcibly cast in normally disabled states.
Can be used when hit / hit in mid-air / swimming / climbing.
Skill Notes — Describe the general effect during editing.
Usage Limit — Number of uses. One use restored per cooldown.
Resource Consumption — Consumption type and amount via Skill Resource.
Skill Attribute Group — Dynamically alter specific skill behaviors.
Destroy the Skill When Reaching the Usage Limit — Auto-remove at max uses.
Usage Limit defines the maximum uses during entire life cycle.
Enable Charging branch — Define transition from combo to charge actions.
Charging Global Pre-movement — Shared animation slot for combo and charge.
Aiming State Activation — Hold (click and hold) or Toggle (tap to enter/exit).
Movement While Aiming — Allow character movement in aiming mode.
Aim & Fire Animation Duration — Modify firing animation length.
After defining casting conditions and logic, click Animation Editor to continue editing
specific logic after skill cast. Different skill types have different configuration methods.
Configuration uses a timeline format with an action track and logic track. When the skill is cast,
animations and events are triggered sequentially based on the timeline.
Animation defines the scale of the entire skill track. Total event timeline equals sum
of all animation durations. Events can be added at corresponding timestamps.
Animation Resource — Replace the current animation asset.
Slot — Change the slot the animation is assigned to.
Slot Duration — Follow Animation or Custom (cannot exceed animation duration).
Supports snapping to animation start/end points (red lines), preview points (blue lines),
and endpoints of Node Graph Event track and Status track.
Allows logic within a specified time range to be executed repeatedly.
Loop Segment Start Time / Duration — Define the loop window.
Infinite Loop — Repeats until timeline end. Number of Loops — Finite repeats.
Supported for: Hold Skills, Normal Skills, Air Skills. Each slot supports both looping
and non-looping animations. Add/remove slots with + icon or right-click.
There is a very small probability that events will be skipped in the event track of a node
graph, so some key logic should be written on the ending event track as a fallback.
Provides looping animation with hold input.
Combo track with pre-input and response phases.
Enter Aiming State + Show Crosshair events.
Cast while airborne at certain height.
Similar to Aiming Skills in use.
Each branch represents multiple bundled animations. During skill casting, the skill transits
into a specific branch based on when the hold is released. Branch tracks define transition
rules with configurable segments and response animations.
Pre-input Phase — Accepts pre-input and triggers transition at first response phase.
Holding during pre-input can transit to charge branch.
Response Phase — Tapping triggers response and animation transition.
Successful Charge Duration — Hold duration required for charge branch entry.
Camera FOV — Field of view in aiming state.
Aiming Camera Offset — Camera position offset.
Pitch Angle Range — Adjustable camera pitch.
Enter/Exit Transition Duration — Camera switching smoothness.
Conditions for entering Landing Branch: while within an "Enter Landing Branch" state block,
landing triggers transition. Multiple "Enter Landing Branch" nodes can be configured.
Event blocks can follow the Infinite Loop slot, repeating indefinitely.
Skill slots determine where skills appear on the UI and what input activates them.
When a skill is added to a unit, a Skill Instance is created with a unique integer ID.
A single unit can have multiple instances with the same skill configuration ID.
A skill must be linked to a slot for UI input; otherwise, only Execution Nodes trigger it.
A single slot can contain multiple instances in a stack. Most recently added is active.
Air Skills and Ground Skills use independent storage structures.
Through Node Graphs, instances can be bound/unbound to/from slots.
Unbound instances remain on the character; cooldown continues to calculate.

svg.text: VI. Adding & Managing Skills Through Node Graphs

g.text: There are two main ways to add skills: (1) default class configuration, (2) via Node Graph.
Trigger skill cast via node execution.
Skills can consume configured Skill Resources. Resource type and amount are defined in
numerical configuration. Resources can be modified and queried through node graphs.
1. Default class configuration — via Classes documentation
2. Via Node Graph — using Modify Skills execution nodes

svg.text: AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
